description: First upstream JSONL found in corpus. Contains 4 thinking blocks and 60 attachment entries — neither exist in the events layer. Triggered the backup feature req.

Heuristic fired build.ruflo_orchestrator but session was building the Rufus skill, not running Ruflo. Confirmed in next batch that a6fde96f IS a real ruflo run (/appydave:ruflo invoked). Two distinct patterns exist.
First confirmed real ruflo orchestration session — /appydave:ruflo invoked, 17 agents, marathon. build.ruflo_orchestrator is a legitimate subtype that needs a taxonomy entry.
Supabase MCP tools appeared for the first time: mcp__supabase__list_projects and mcp__supabase__execute_sql. SupportSignal story 10.22 used MCP to inspect the database directly during orchestration.
kiros-sentinal project appeared for the first time — 401-event marathon building an owna-collector feature. David mentioned hoping to see Ruflo used in it.

David was mid-session designing the classification taxonomy while running enrichment — asking whether sessions should have multiple subtypes, what primary vs secondary means. The system was being designed from within itself.

build.test_writing fired on a brains marathon session about Dark Factory Orchestration System design. No test writing occurred — 285 events of methodology design and brain writing. Heuristic pattern likely matched on file extensions incorrectly.
skill.development heuristic appeared on an angeleye session running mocha loop (enrich-subtypes, ScheduleWakeup x19). Not in taxonomy. Session is better classified as build.campaign.
skill.creation heuristic appeared on a marathon angeleye session running enrich-subtypes. Not in taxonomy. Second unlisted heuristic value after skill.development.

All appyctrl ghost/scheduled_probe sessions are caused by T3/OpenCode's Claude provider driver. apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeProvider.ts:445 (probeClaudeCapabilities) spawns Claude every 5 minutes (SNAPSHOT_REFRESH_INTERVAL) with an intentionally infinite-wait prompt generator — it just reads init data (account info, slash commands) then aborts. AngelEye hooks fire SessionStart + InstructionsLoaded + SessionEnd, no UserPromptSubmit. Expect ~288/day, ~2000/week of these while appyctrl is running. Not user-initiated. Should likely be filtered out at ingestion (is_junk = true) rather than enriched.

First post-junk-filter batch: 0 silent sessions, 100% real work. Filter is doing its job. Across 100 sessions: 8 BMAD orchestrator runs (stories 0.41-0.47, 10.12), 2 Ralphy autonomous campaigns (one with 996 events of Playwright UI testing), 6 system-context orientations, 3 focus-skill research/methodology runs.

Heavy use of mcp__playwright__browser_* tools (165 click + 150 snapshot + 110 screenshots). Playwright MCP is now a major tool class for E2E verification within Ralphy campaigns. Cross-checks with the schema_anomaly observation about T3 — different host integration patterns.
19 CronCreate tool calls in a single session — David scheduled many recurring tasks during a Baku organisation campaign. CronCreate is a substantial Claude tool not yet in any classifier rule.

als-workflows session has 1299 events — the largest session in this batch. Marathon-scale orchestration covering FliLaunch build, project analysis, FliHub status disputes. AngelEye has no special UI treatment for sessions of this scale.

Many new projects appeared in this batch: kiros-quality-portal, b-kiros, dublin, appyfinance, screentour, agent-ui, agentic-os, app-ideas, supportsignal-sentinal, sentinal.supportsignal, appyradar-sentinel, als-test, als-workflows, lars, appydave-plugins, apps, beauty-and-joy, voz, awb. The corpus represents a large polyrepo workspace.

David ran /focus prompt-patterns specifically asking about self-improving and learning systems for prompts and agents. Direct ancestor of the dreaming-skill conversation we had today — he was already thinking about it weeks ago.

Heuristic build.ci_pipeline appeared (Epic 10 retrospective for SupportSignal). Not in taxonomy. Session was actually a knowledge.brain_capture (writing up the retro), not CI work. Heuristic is misfiring on the word 'pipeline' in the prompt.

build.iterative_design heuristic appeared multiple times this batch (kiros-quality-portal session). Not in taxonomy. Recurring gap — would auto-promote in dreaming after 3+ occurrences (already past that threshold).
orientation.artifact_lookup heuristic appeared multiple times. Not in taxonomy. Different from orientation.file_retrieval (artifact = build artifact, not just any file). Worth distinguishing.

BMAD story velocity: this batch alone contains stories 0.31, 0.33, 0.34, 0.39, 0.40, 11, 11.1, 11.6, 11.7, 11.8, 11.10, 11.11, 11.12. SupportSignal is on story 11+ which means epic 11 is well underway. Could be a useful UI signal — show story velocity over time per project.

First /bmad-pm invocation seen — different from /appydave:bmad-story-lifecycle. PM agent for PRD building. The bmad-* family of agents (pm, sm, dev, dr, e0, ux-designer) are individual agent invocations vs the orchestrator lifecycle. Worth distinguishing in classifier.
claude-certified-architect project appeared — 402 events, 221 prompts. David doing intensive iterative work on a new system. First time this project is in corpus.

7 sessions this batch start with handover pastes from previous conversations (typical pattern: 'I'm continuing from another conversation', 'paste this to start', 'Open a new Claude Code window in...'). Cross-session continuation is heavy. A has_handover_paste predicate would let you query for these explicitly.

New projects in this batch: lars-brain (3 sessions, brain work), growth-intelligence (2 sessions, build.feature), signal-studio (2 sessions). Three new corners of David's polyrepo workspace appearing for the first time.

Heavy cross-window continuation pattern in this batch — multiple kiros-quality-portal Phase 3, 5, 6 handovers, supportsignal continuation pastes. Confirms the has_handover_paste predicate would be high-value (was already noted last batch).

David named the four pillars vocabulary explicitly today: canonical source of truth, provenance chain, synthesis, citation. truth-trail skill already enforces these. The quality-scan-2026-05-07.md report I produced violates all four — counts came from skills.json (deterministic, citable) but kind/family/duplicate inferences were vibe-judgments. Future generated artifacts should separate deterministic from interpretive layers and cite sources.

New projects in this batch: wui (3 quick check sessions), harness (1 marathon campaign), scripts (2 sessions). Continuing the pattern of new projects appearing every batch as we go further back into the corpus.

awb dominates this batch with 22 sessions (18 heavy build.campaign + 2 marathon + 2 file_retrieval). Many are Ralphy autonomous campaigns. AWB development is in a high-velocity phase during this corpus window.

VIDEO TRUTH-CHECK: David suspected his Ruflo video misrepresented what was happening. Investigated this session (the canonical 'real Ruflo' run we tagged). Verdict: zero claude-flow CLI invocations in Bash commands. The session ran /appydave:ruflo's install/maintenance workflow only (refresh-upstream, install, commit, memory-init, status, prune), then pivoted to AppyCtrl feature building using 17 regular Claude Code Task subagents (Agent tool) and delivery-review. NO actual Ruflo Swarm execution. David's own prompts (P26, P31, P38, P39) explicitly express uncertainty about which mechanism was being used. The video, if it claimed Ruflo Swarm execution, was showing Claude Code Task subagents — not Ruflo. CITATION: events file for session a6fde96f-eef3-4ada-a1c4-2c3a285bb2fe; Bash command grep for claude-flow|swarm|npx returned 0 matches; prompt count = 42, Agent tool count = 17, claude-flow CLI count = 0.

Detection rule for distinguishing Ruflo mechanisms going forward: (1) Real Ruflo Swarm = Bash commands matching /claude-flow|npx claude-flow|swarm init/. (2) Agent Teams = teammate_id field set, session_kind=subagent. (3) Task subagents = Agent tool calls in events. (4) Wrapper-only = /appydave:ruflo invocation but zero claude-flow Bash calls — what we previously wrongly tagged as 'real Ruflo'. The taxonomy and skills-registry detection for ruflo should distinguish wrapper-invocation from actual-swarm-execution.

prompt.supportsignal.com.au project appearing in this batch — 10 sessions with mostly orientation/codebase exploration. Distinct from app.supportsignal.com.au. The SupportSignal v2 satellite repo described in the supportsignal-planning-sync skill.

Heavy orientation phase: 37 of 100 sessions classified as orientation.codebase_exploration — this corpus window is dominated by reading/exploration over building. Suggests we are in a pre-active-development time period or a major handover/onboarding phase.

Three sessions in this batch have project field set to a UUID instead of a real project name (cfcc0c4b-9da7-4efd-9cd3-e83c3b3adb57, 8fd2ea7b-2e4c-4d16-ab25-081937f39e4c, 54ea7cf7-e406-4042-85ec-066c58e61c12). This suggests an ingestion bug where the session_id leaked into the project field. Should be investigated — likely affects more sessions across the corpus.

Two main+non-junk sessions have project='undefined' (literal string) and project_dir='undefined'. Likely env-var miss during background/detached process startup. Added as E6 to the escapes ledger. Total corpus count: 2 sessions.

ENRICHMENT QUEUE CLEARED. Final state: 2438 total sessions / 1050 junk-filtered / 1150 main enriched / 0 remaining. The full historic corpus is now classified at v1. Dreaming pass now becomes the next move — reflection over the 1150 enrichments will surface aggregate patterns the per-batch analysis missed.

Ralphy sessions have session_subtype='build.campaign' in the registry, not 'build.ralphy_campaign' as the classifier code (per workflow-infrastructure-research) suggested. Either classifier was updated, or the mapping happens in a layer the research missed. Anyone filtering on 'build.ralphy_campaign' gets zero hits. Discoverable filter is 'build.campaign' + trigger_command =~ /ralphy/.

angeleye-retrieve cannot distinguish 'session ABOUT a skill' (e.g. 0d6cbb83 in appydave-plugins, build.feature) from 'session DRIVEN BY a skill' (build.campaign + trigger=ralphy). Both surface on keyword search. A future enhancement: weight matches on trigger_command higher than matches on first_real_prompt content, and surface trigger_command explicitly in output to let the reader distinguish.

First successful use of angeleye-retrieve produced docs/intelligence/ralphy-synthesis.md — paired the codified appydave:ralphy SKILL.md (~815 lines of intent) with 74 corpus sessions (lived experience). This skill+corpus+gap pattern generalises to any David-authored workflow. Worth becoming a routine for Ruflo, BMAD, Mochaccino, Paperclip-as-harness, AppyCtrl.

RUFLO CORPUS-WIDE VERDICT: Across 1,161 main non-junk sessions (and a focused 101-session sweep across appyctrl|kiros-sentinal|angeleye|appysentinal|kiros-quality), there are ZERO actual `npx claude-flow` CLI invocations and ZERO mcp__ruv-swarm__* / mcp__claude-flow__* tool calls. The wrapper-without-swarm pattern from ruflo-investigation.md is universal, not just confined to tagged sessions. 8 ruflo-keyword sessions exist, all in a 3-day window (2026-05-04 to 2026-05-06), and only 1 (a6fde96f) actually invokes /appydave:ruflo as designed. Citation: docs/intelligence/ruflo-synthesis.md.

trigger_command field is NULL on every Ruflo-related session in the corpus, including a6fde96f which explicitly invokes `/appydave:ruflo` in its prompts. The classifier is detecting Ralphy invocations (trigger_command =~ /ralphy/ surfaces 74 sessions) but not Ruflo invocations. Likely a missing case in the trigger detection — should fire on /ruflo and /appydave:ruflo same way it fires on /ralphy. This makes Ruflo invisible to trigger_command-based filters.

Second skill+corpus+gap synthesis (after Ralphy). Ruflo synthesis demonstrates the pattern produces value even on a NEGATIVE result — the skill is correctly codified, but the corpus shows zero adoption of its execution path. This is more useful to David than a confirming result because it surfaces the activation-energy problem (skill exists, install done, but 'spawn a swarm' trigger never typed). The pattern is now confirmed for both high-volume (Ralphy, 74 sessions) and low-volume (Ruflo, 8 sessions) archetypes. Next archetypes worth synthesising: BMAD (likely high-volume), Mochaccino (medium), Paperclip-as-harness, AppyCtrl.

AppyCtrl synthesis: 1,007 silent sessions corpus-wide, 651 (65%) tagged to project=appyctrl, 100% correctly is_junk=true. Cadence verified at exactly 5min intervals matching SNAPSHOT_REFRESH_INTERVAL = Duration.minutes(5) in apps/server/src/provider/Drivers/ClaudeDriver.ts:40. The corpus empirically confirms the source code's probe loop. AppyCtrl is the only harness archetype that runs continuously and produces ~99% noise + 1% signal.

Silent sessions inherit cwd of whatever AppyCtrl tab the user has open — so 320 of the 1,007 silent sessions are tagged project=app.supportsignal.com.au not because SupportSignal is probing but because that's where the user was browsing in T3 Code at probe time. Anyone doing per-project analytics on meta.silent_session rows would draw wrong conclusions. Reframing question logged: should these become harness_heartbeat events with explicit harness_id, vs staying as observable-pattern junk?

AppyCtrl Phase 3.1 build session was misclassified by heuristic as ruflo_orchestrator even though no Ruflo skill was invoked — same over-eager-Ruflo pattern surfaced in Ralphy synthesis (build.campaign vs build.ralphy_campaign mismatch). LLM enrichment corrected to build.campaign. Heuristic needs a single review pass once 3-4 such cases accumulate.

BMAD classifier backfill missing. Commit f79a000 (2026-05-06) added build.bmad_orchestrator + build.bmad_agent subtypes but applies prospectively only. Corpus has 84 orchestrator sessions (trigger=appydave:bmad-story-lifecycle); only 5 carry the new subtype. 79 still show build.campaign or other. All 267 phase agent sessions still carry old subtypes — zero have build.bmad_agent. A re-classify pass on prior data would close a 94% miss on 'all BMAD orchestrator sessions by subtype' queries. Citation: docs/intelligence/bmad-synthesis.md.

BMAD Mode-3 silent fallback. 12 of 84 orchestrator sessions (14%) invoked /appydave:bmad-story-lifecycle but spawned ZERO agents — Swagger embodied every phase itself (e.g. d3b14ea3: 61 Edits, 57 Bash, 28 Playwright, 0 Agent calls). The skill prescribes 'Swagger never embodies agents' as a non-negotiable. Enrichment notes call these 'standard orchestrator flow' — they're not. A diagnostics-page tile listing 'BMAD runs that bypassed the agent harness this week' would surface the regression. Easy detection: trigger=appydave:bmad-story-lifecycle AND zero Agent tool calls.

BMAD Mode 2 → Mode 1 transition is visible in corpus. 2026-03-29 to 2026-04-05: 5–58 phase-agent sessions per day, 0–4 orchestrator. 2026-04-06 onward: 0–4 phase-agent per day, 1–13 orchestrator. The discontinuity is clean. Mode 2 (visible iTerm2 panes) era produced 1 orch + ~9 phase sessions per story; Mode 1 (Agent Teams in-process) era collapses the same lifecycle into 1 orch session with N Agent tool calls. 36 of 69 Mode-1 orchestrator sessions (52%) have 7-9 Agent calls — strong corpus support for codified ~9-phase shape. A computed bmad_execution_mode field (1|2|3) inferable from TeamCreate/Agent/SendMessage tool counts would let downstream queries distinguish 'did this run use the right harness?'.
